query: I'm about to merge feature/billing-engine into develop. Run the full quality gate — tests, completeness scan, code review, the works.
run quality checks on my changes before I commit. make sure there are no stubs or TODOs left
scan my code for completeness — I want to make sure nothing is a placeholder or mock implementation before this goes to staging
note: Completeness gate is a sub-step of quality-pipeline

query: quality gate please. this is an L-size feature so I need the full pipeline: test, scan, completeness, review
note: Direct invocation with explicit L-size route

query: this is a hotfix for the scheduler panic. run quality checks — but skip completeness since it's a hotfix, just tests and review
note: Hotfix route — quality-pipeline knows to skip scan/completeness

query: write unit tests for the calculate_monthly_settlement function covering edge cases: zero balance, negative commission, cross-tenant split
note: Writing tests — superpowers:test-driven-development, not quality-pipeline (which runs existing tests)

query: the CI pipeline is failing on the lint step. Can you look at the GitHub Actions log and figure out why?
note: Debugging CI failure — superpowers:systematic-debugging, not quality-pipeline

query: I got review comments from my teammate on PR #87. Help me evaluate whether their suggestions about error handling are valid.
note: Receiving external code review — superpowers:receiving-code-review, not quality-pipeline

query: refactor the test suite in backend/src/accounting/tests/ to use shared fixtures instead of duplicating setup code
note: Test refactoring — direct implementation work, not quality-pipeline

query: run cargo clippy on the backend and fix all the warnings
note: Direct linting command — no skill needed, just run the command
